title: cmate-orchestrate merge report v1
description: Report printed to stdout and written to merge-report.json by the cmate-orchestrate merge runner (scripts/merge.mjs). Closed schema: an unknown field is a contract violation, not something a reader may ignore. It records one mutating phase — either PR creation (create_prs) or guarded merge (merge_prs) — over the verification-passed issues of an already-executed plan. A single invocation enables exactly one phase. Nothing is created or merged without explicit approval, and no PR is merged unless its CI passed; a create failure, a red or pending CI, or a merge conflict stops the phase and is reported as partial, never rounded up to success.

phase.description: The single mutating phase this invocation enabled. create_prs opens a PR per verification-passed issue; merge_prs checks CI and, if green and approved, merges. Exactly one is ever enabled.

status.description: success: the phase handled every eligible issue without a failure (including a no-mutation preview). partial: the phase started but stopped early on a PR-create failure, a red/pending CI, a missing PR, or a merge conflict. failure: nothing could be attempted (preflight failed, no plan, or a bad invocation).

stop_reason.description: Why the phase stopped. completed: every eligible issue was handled. The rest name the first blocking condition; a failure is never reported as completed.

approved.description: Whether --approve was given. When false the phase is a no-mutation preview: no branch is pushed, no PR is created, and no PR is merged.

mutated.description: Whether at least one mutating command (git push, gh pr create, or gh pr merge) was actually invoked. Always false when approved is false.

merge_method.description: The merge method used (or that would be used) for the merge_prs phase. Reported in both phases for completeness.

out_dir.description: Directory the merge artifacts were written to. Null when nothing was written.

eligible_issues.description: Issues drawn from the dispatch report whose worker completed AND whose verification passed. Only these are ever acted on: an unverified issue is never turned into a PR or a merge.

preflight.description: Read-only preflight probes run before any mutation (gh availability, repo access, base resolvable). A blocking failure yields status failure and nothing is attempted.

targets.description: One record per eligible issue, in merge order. A target reached after the phase stopped is recorded with outcome skipped so the full set stays visible.

eligible.description: True: this issue's worker completed and its verification passed. Only eligible issues appear as targets.

pushed.description: Whether the branch was pushed (create_prs phase only; always false in a preview or in merge_prs).

ci_passed.description: Whether every required check reported success. A merge is refused unless this is true — completion never opens the merge gate on its own.
